query: Is our running-account settlement policy compliant as of today, and has anything changed in the underlying rules?
notes: scope valid obligations (T1), find settlement obligation (T2), trace supersession/consolidation (T4), confirm the superseding item is effective (T1), rank (T5). The supersession trace is the whole point.

query: How often must running accounts be settled?
notes: simple lookup; no supersession trace needed.

query: What's the position on client money sitting in our account?
notes: lay phrasing 'client money' is ambiguous; CRAG correction should reformulate to canonical SEBI terms (running-account settlement / upstreaming of client funds) and re-retrieve.

query: What obligations apply when an authorised person collects funds from clients?
notes: direct topical lookup -> AP conduct obligations.

query: Can we earn a bit of interest on the client money we are sitting on?
notes: lay "earn interest on client money" -> canonical: client funds may only be invested in eligible MFOS (G-Sec/TREPS). Naive retrieval MISSES SB-UPSTREAM-008 (not in top-10; top-1 RUNACCT-003); reformulation to "investment of client funds / MFOS" required.

query: Our sub-broker wants to collect cash and shares straight from clients, is that allowed?
notes: lay "sub-broker" -> canonical "authorized person". Naive retrieval MISSES SB-RUNACCT-010 (top-1 UPSTREAM-002); reformulate to "authorized person accepting client funds/securities".

query: A client let us operate their account, can we use that to shuffle their shares for our own needs?
notes: lay "let us operate their account" -> canonical "power of attorney (PoA)". Naive retrieval gives WRONG top-1 (RUNACCT-011); SB-EWMDS-019 only at rank 3. Reformulate to "client power of attorney restricted to settlement".

query: A client keeps missing their share delivery deadlines, what are we required to do?
notes: lay "missing delivery deadlines" -> canonical "repeated pay-in shortages". Naive retrieval WRONG top-1 (CLTSEC-005), SB-EWMDS-005 at pool rank 10 / rerank rank 2. Reformulate to "pay-in shortage monitoring".

query: running account settlement calendar issued by stock exchanges
notes: already canonical; SB-RUNACCT-002 retrieves at top-1. No correction needed.

query: auto-release of pledge if not invoked or released within seven trading days
notes: already canonical; SB-CLTSEC-009 retrieves at top-1. No correction needed.

query: F&O risk disclosure minimum screen area of fifty percent
notes: already canonical; SB-RISKDISC-003 retrieves at top-1. No correction needed.
